summary: G4 plan step: build_dossiers.py --plan span packing + committed freeze artifact (span_plan_c1)
description: Spans: natural sections (trust parsed strings), merge to span_target, split oversized to '(part n)', budget-pack fallback for bare-format texts; degenerate flag for single-span works; fold/map-reduce nodes only when input_budget>0. Slug rules per implementation doc. Output data/span_plan_c1.json + docs/summary/span-plan-c1.md, committed (V9 freeze). CH work's freeze gated by c59758f3. Done: tests/test_span_plan.py (every chunk in exactly one span, corpus order, known cases incl. 14 degenerate works).

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Spec tension: work-grouping.md planner notes say tiny members (Dhammapada chapters ~1.5k) merge up to span_target across member texts, but the design's L1 id scheme sum:{text_id}:{span_slug} requires each span to live in exactly one text, and cross-member spans would also blur structure_json's per-chapter TOC. Resolution: spans NEVER cross member-text boundaries — a tiny member is simply a small span. Cost: ~300 extra small L1 calls corpus-wide, negligible under claude-code; benefit: id integrity, per-chapter structure entries (better reader-facing TOC), simpler plan. Within-text section merging up to span_target still applies.

resolution.note: Plan mode + freeze artifact committed (docs/summary/span-plan-c1.{json,md} — data/ is gitignored so the machine artifact lives beside the md): 52 works, 623 spans, 11 degenerate, 0 folds under claude-code; folds verified to activate under input_budget=6000 (plotinus >=2). corpus-hermeticum flagged GATED by c59758f3 in the artifact. Spec deviation recorded as analysis[0]: spans never cross member texts. 9/9 tests.
